name: Nacho Potato Skins
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare 1 cup of any combination of the listed cheeses.
2. Thoroughly scrub potatoes with a vegetable brush and pat dry.
3. Optionally, for soft skins, rub potatoes with shortening, margarine, or butter; prick with a fork.
- Alternatively, for moist skins, skip the shortening rub and prick with a fork.
4. Bake potatoes in a 425°F oven for 40 to 60 minutes or until tender.
5. Cut lengthwise into quarters and scoop out the potato pulp, leaving a 1/4-inch thick shell.
6. Reserve the potato pulp for another use (e.g., mashed potatoes).
7. Brush both sides of the potato skins with the melted margarine.
8. Sprinkle seasoned salt and ground red pepper over the inside of each skin.
9. Place potato pieces, skin side up, on an unheated rack in a broiler pan.
10. Broil 3 to 4 inches from heat for 3 minutes.
11. Turn potato pieces skin side down and sprinkle with shredded cheese.
12. Continue broiling for 2 more minutes.
13. Arrange potato pieces on a heated serving platter.
14. Optionally, garnish with a red chili pepper and cilantro sprig; serve with desired toppings.
Makes 24 appetizer servings.
